text:
MWS Octapi LowCode Lua platform rules:
- Lua 5.5 is used.
- Scripts are embedded as JsonString: lua{ -- code }lua
- All workflow variables: wf.vars.VARNAME (dot-notation, NOT JsonPath).
- Startup/input variables: wf.initVariables.VARNAME.
- To create a new array: _utils.array.new()
- To mark an existing table as array: _utils.array.markAsArray(arr)
- Allowed types: nil, boolean, number, string, table, function, array.
- Allowed constructs: if/then/else, while/do/end, for/do/end, repeat/until.
- Do NOT use os.*, io.*, require(), dofile(), loadfile().
- Use `return` to produce the value. Do NOT use print().

MWS Octapi LowCode JSON wrapper format:
Lua scripts are embedded within JSON values using the format:
"fieldName": "lua{ <lua code here> }lua"

Examples:
{"result": "lua{return wf.vars.myValue}lua"}
{"count": "lua{return wf.vars.counter + 1}lua"}

The lua code inside the wrapper should use return to produce the value that will be assigned to the JSON field.
Multiple fields can each have their own lua block.

IMPORTANT: When generating code, return ONLY the bare Lua code without the lua{...}lua wrapper.

Task: Из полученного списка email получи последний.
Context: wf.vars.emails is an array of email strings.
Lua code:
return wf.vars.emails[#wf.vars.emails]

Task: Увеличивай значение переменной try_count_n на каждой итерации.
Context: wf.vars.try_count_n is a number.
Lua code:
return wf.vars.try_count_n + 1

Task: Очисти значения переменных ID, ENTITY_ID, CALL из REST-ответа (оставить только эти поля).
Context: wf.vars.RESTbody.result is an array of tables with various keys.
Lua code:
result = wf.vars.RESTbody.result
for _, filteredEntry in pairs(result) do
  for key, value in pairs(filteredEntry) do
    if key ~= "ID" and key ~= "ENTITY_ID" and key ~= "CALL" then
      filteredEntry[key] = nil
    end
  end
end
return result

Task: Преобразуй время из формата YYYYMMDD и HHMMSS в строку ISO 8601.
Context: wf.vars.json.IDOC.ZCDF_HEAD.DATUM = 'YYYYMMDD', wf.vars.json.IDOC.ZCDF_HEAD.TIME = 'HHMMSS'.
Lua code:
DATUM = wf.vars.json.IDOC.ZCDF_HEAD.DATUM
TIME = wf.vars.json.IDOC.ZCDF_HEAD.TIME
local function safe_sub(str, start, finish)
  local s = string.sub(str, start, math.min(finish, #str))
  return s ~= "" and s or "00"
end
year = safe_sub(DATUM, 1, 4)
month = safe_sub(DATUM, 5, 6)
day = safe_sub(DATUM, 7, 8)
hour = safe_sub(TIME, 1, 2)
minute = safe_sub(TIME, 3, 4)
second = safe_sub(TIME, 5, 6)
iso_date = string.format('%s-%s-%sT%s:%s:%s.00000Z', year, month, day, hour, minute, second)
return iso_date

Task: Преобразуй структуру так, чтобы все items в ZCDF_PACKAGES всегда были массивами.
Context: wf.vars.json.IDOC.ZCDF_HEAD.ZCDF_PACKAGES — массив пакетов, items может быть объектом или массивом.
Lua code:
function ensureArray(t)
  if type(t) ~= "table" then
    return {t}
  end
  local isArray = true
  for k, v in pairs(t) do
    if type(k) ~= "number" or math.floor(k) ~= k then
      isArray = false
      break
    end
  end
  return isArray and t or {t}
end
function ensureAllItemsAreArrays(objectsArray)
  if type(objectsArray) ~= "table" then
    return objectsArray
  end
  for _, obj in ipairs(objectsArray) do
    if type(obj) == "table" and obj.items then
      obj.items = ensureArray(obj.items)
    end
  end
  return objectsArray
end
return ensureAllItemsAreArrays(wf.vars.json.IDOC.ZCDF_HEAD.ZCDF_PACKAGES)

Task: Отфильтруй элементы массива — включи только те, у которых есть Discount или Markdown.
Context: wf.vars.parsedCsv — массив таблиц с полями SKU, Discount, Markdown.
Lua code:
local result = _utils.array.new()
local items = wf.vars.parsedCsv
for _, item in ipairs(items) do
  if (item.Discount ~= "" and item.Discount ~= nil) or (item.Markdown ~= "" and item.Markdown ~= nil) then
    table.insert(result, item)
  end
end
return result

Task: Добавь переменную с квадратом числа.
Context: число задано как строка '5'.
Lua code:
local n = tonumber('5')
return n * n

Task: Конвертируй время в переменной recallTime в unix-формат.
Context: wf.initVariables.recallTime содержит ISO 8601 дату '2023-10-15T15:30:00+00:00'.
Lua code:
local iso_time = wf.initVariables.recallTime
local days_in_month = {31, 28, 31, 30, 31, 30, 31, 31, 30, 31, 30, 31}
if not iso_time or not iso_time:match("^%d%d%d%d%-%d%d%-%d%dT") then
  return nil
end
local function is_leap_year(year)
  return (year % 4 == 0 and year % 100 ~= 0) or (year % 400 == 0)
end
local function days_since_epoch(year, month, day)
  local days = 0
  for y = 1970, year - 1 do
    days = days + (is_leap_year(y) and 366 or 365)
  end
  for m = 1, month - 1 do
    days = days + days_in_month[m]
    if m == 2 and is_leap_year(year) then
      days = days + 1
    end
  end
  days = days + (day - 1)
  return days
end
local function parse_iso8601_to_epoch(iso_str)
  if not iso_str then error("Date is nil") end
  local year, month, day, hour, min, sec, ms, offset_sign, offset_hour, offset_min =
    iso_str:match("(%d+)-(%d+)-(%d+)T(%d+):(%d+):(%d+)%.(%d+)([+-])(%d+):(%d+)")
  if not year then
    year, month, day, hour, min, sec, offset_sign, offset_hour, offset_min =
      iso_str:match("(%d+)-(%d+)-(%d+)T(%d+):(%d+):(%d+)([+-])(%d+):(%d+)")
    ms = 0
  end
  if not year then error("Cannot parse date: " .. tostring(iso_str)) end
  year = tonumber(year); month = tonumber(month); day = tonumber(day)
  hour = tonumber(hour); min = tonumber(min); sec = tonumber(sec)
  offset_hour = tonumber(offset_hour); offset_min = tonumber(offset_min)
  local total_days = days_since_epoch(year, month, day)
  local total_seconds = total_days * 86400 + hour * 3600 + min * 60 + sec
  local offset_seconds = offset_hour * 3600 + offset_min * 60
  if offset_sign == "-" then offset_seconds = -offset_seconds end
  return total_seconds - offset_seconds
end
local epoch_seconds = parse_iso8601_to_epoch(iso_time)
return epoch_seconds

Common Lua pattern: Accessing the last element of an array.
Use #array to get length, then index: array[#array]
Example: return wf.vars.items[#wf.vars.items]

Common Lua pattern: Filtering an array by condition.
Create a new result array, iterate with ipairs, insert matching items:
local result = _utils.array.new()
for _, item in ipairs(wf.vars.source) do
  if <condition> then
    table.insert(result, item)
  end
end
return result

Common Lua pattern: Removing fields from tables in an array.
Iterate the array, for each entry iterate its keys, set unwanted keys to nil:
for _, entry in pairs(array) do
  for key, _ in pairs(entry) do
    if key ~= "keep1" and key ~= "keep2" then
      entry[key] = nil
    end
  end
end

Common Lua pattern: Checking if a value is non-empty.
A value is considered 'present' if it is not nil AND not empty string:
if value ~= nil and value ~= "" then ... end

For null/nil checking: if value == nil then ... end

Common Lua pattern: String parsing with string.sub and string.format.
Extract substrings by position: string.sub(str, start, finish)
Format output: string.format('%s-%s', part1, part2)
Pattern matching: string.match(str, pattern) with Lua patterns.

Common Lua pattern: Type checking and ensuring array type.
function ensureArray(t)
  if type(t) ~= "table" then return {t} end
  for k in pairs(t) do
    if type(k) ~= "number" then return {t} end
  end
  return t
end

Lua string functions reference:
- string.sub(s, i, j): substring from i to j
- string.format(fmt, ...): formatted output
- string.match(s, pattern): pattern matching, returns captures
- string.find(s, pattern): find pattern position
- string.gsub(s, pattern, repl): global substitution
- #s: string length
- string.upper(s), string.lower(s): case conversion
- string.rep(s, n): repeat string

Lua table functions reference:
- table.insert(t, value): append element
- table.remove(t, pos): remove element at position
- table.sort(t, comp): sort in-place
- table.concat(t, sep): concatenate with separator
- #t: array length
- pairs(t): iterate all key-value pairs
- ipairs(t): iterate array part (1, 2, 3, ...)

Lua math and type functions:
- math.floor(x), math.ceil(x): rounding
- math.abs(x), math.max(...), math.min(...)
- math.fmod(x, y): modulo
- tonumber(s): string to number (nil on failure)
- tostring(v): any value to string
- type(v): returns type name as string

Lua pattern matching characters:
%d=digit, %a=letter, %w=alphanumeric, %s=whitespace, %p=punctuation
.=any char, *=0+ greedy, +=1+, -=0+ lazy, ?=0 or 1
^=start, $=end, ()=captures
Example: str:match("(%d+)-(%d+)-(%d+)") extracts year-month-day
